consistency.level_and_damage_scaling: Consistent. Base damage is 12d6 at 7th level, scaling by +1d6 per slot level above 7th, which is standard for high-level area control/damage spells.
consistency.component_notes: The material component (bat guano and sulfur) strongly evokes the traditional Fireball, reinforcing the theme, even though this spell is delayed.

quality.description_clarity: High. The description clearly delineates the setup (glowing bead), the detonation conditions (concentration ends, choice to end, or being touched), and the resulting explosion mechanics (Dex save, damage).
quality.flavor_and_mechanics_synergy: Excellent. The mechanics perfectly capture the fantasy of preparing an explosion and timing it. The mechanic of increased damage over time rewards patience, while the risk/reward of touching it adds tactical depth.
quality.readability: Good, though the multiple detonation conditions require careful reading.

mechanics.core_effect: Delayed area-of-effect fire damage (20ft radius sphere).
unique_mechanics: Delayed Detonation: Damage increases if the caster maintains concentration (1d6 per turn after the caster's turn).
Interaction Risk: Touching the bead forces a save; failure detonates it immediately on the toucher. Success allows the bead to be thrown (up to 40ft) before detonation.
mechanics.save_and_damage: Dexterity saving throw for half damage, standard for large AoE fire spells.
mechanics.concentration_tradeoff: The main strategic choice is maintaining concentration to increase damage (up to a potential 12d6 + 7d6 = 19d6 if concentration lasts 7 rounds) versus the risk of losing concentration or choosing to detonate early for immediate effect.
